name: Nmap
description: The gold standard network scanner. Host discovery, port scanning, service/version detection, OS fingerprinting.
longDescription: Nmap (Network Mapper) is a free and open-source utility for network discovery and security auditing. It uses raw IP packets to determine available hosts, services, operating systems, packet filters/firewalls, and dozens of other characteristics. It was designed to rapidly scan large networks but works fine against single hosts. Nmap's scripting engine (NSE) allows users to write and share scripts to automate networking tasks, from vulnerability detection to backdoor discovery. It's used by security professionals, system administrators, and researchers worldwide.

installCommands.from source: git clone https://github.com/nmap/nmap.git && cd nmap && ./configure && make && sudo make install

useCases: Host discovery and port scanning across network ranges
Service version detection and OS fingerprinting
githubDescription: Nmap - the Network Mapper. Github mirror of official SVN repository.

name: Masscan
description: Internet-scale port scanner. Transmits 10 million packets per second. Asynchronous, stateless scanning.
longDescription: Masscan is the fastest Internet port scanner. It can scan the entire Internet in under 6 minutes, transmitting 10 million packets per second from a single machine. It produces results similar to Nmap but operates asynchronously, transmitting packets without waiting for responses. The internal architecture is more similar to scanrand and unicornscan than traditional scanners. It uses a custom TCP/IP stack to avoid the overhead of the operating system's networking stack.
useCases: Rapid scanning of large IP ranges
Feeding results into Nmap for detailed service detection
githubDescription: TCP port scanner, spews SYN packets asynchronously, scanning entire Internet in under 5 minutes.

name: RustScan
description: Blazing fast port scanner that pipes into Nmap. Scans all 65k ports in 3 seconds flat.
longDescription: RustScan is a lightning-fast port scanner written in Rust, capable of scanning all 65,536 ports in just a few seconds. It leverages Rust's concurrency features to achieve high-speed performance and integrates seamlessly with Nmap for detailed service enumeration and vulnerability detection. RustScan's efficiency makes it ideal for quick reconnaissance activities and large-scale network assessments. Its flexibility and speed set it apart in the realm of network scanning tools.
githubDescription: 🤖 The Modern Port Scanner 🤖

name: Subfinder
description: Fast passive subdomain enumeration tool. Uses multiple sources including certificate transparency logs.
longDescription: Subfinder is a subdomain discovery tool that returns valid subdomains for websites using passive online sources. It has a simple modular architecture and is optimized for speed. Subfinder uses multiple sources including certificate transparency logs, search engines, DNS aggregators, and more to find subdomains without ever touching the target directly. This makes it ideal for stealthy reconnaissance.
useCases: Passive subdomain enumeration for attack surface mapping
Feeding results into httpx for live host probing
githubDescription: Fast passive subdomain enumeration tool.

name: Amass
description: In-depth attack surface mapping and asset discovery. DNS enumeration, network mapping, OSINT data sources.
longDescription: Amass is a powerful tool for attack surface mapping and asset discovery, specializing in DNS enumeration and network mapping. Written in Go, it uses open-source intelligence (OSINT) data sources to uncover subdomains, IP address ranges, and network infrastructure. Amass's comprehensive approach and support for various data sources make it an essential tool for security researchers and penetration testers to identify potential vulnerabilities in exposed assets.
githubDescription: In-depth attack surface mapping and asset discovery

name: httpx
description: Fast multi-purpose HTTP toolkit. Probes for running HTTP servers with retries and fallbacks.
longDescription: Httpx is a fast and versatile HTTP toolkit designed for probing and analyzing HTTP servers. It efficiently handles retries and fallbacks to ensure comprehensive server detection and supports a wide range of features like URL filtering and request concurrency. Built in Go, Httpx is optimized for performance and is part of the ProjectDiscovery suite, making it a valuable asset for security professionals conducting web reconnaissance and HTTP-based assessments.
githubDescription: httpx is a fast and multi-purpose HTTP toolkit that allows running multiple probes using the retryablehttp library.

name: Nuclei
description: Fast vulnerability scanner driven by YAML templates. Thousands of community-contributed detection templates.
longDescription: Nuclei is a fast, customizable vulnerability scanner based on YAML templates. It allows scanning for vulnerabilities, misconfigurations, exposed panels, and more across multiple protocols including HTTP, DNS, TCP, SSL, and JavaScript. The community maintains thousands of detection templates covering CVEs, default credentials, exposed APIs, and technology fingerprints. Nuclei's template system makes it easy to write custom checks and share them with the community.
useCases: Automated vulnerability scanning with YAML templates
Custom security checks for CI/CD pipelines
githubDescription: Nuclei is a fast, customizable vulnerability scanner powered by the global security community and built on a simple YAML-based DSL, enabling collaboration to tackle trending vulnerabilities on the internet. It helps you find vulnerabilities in your applications, APIs, networks, DNS, and cloud configurations.

name: Nikto
description: Classic web server scanner. Tests for dangerous files, outdated server software, and version-specific problems.
longDescription: Nikto is a classic open-source web server scanner that identifies potentially dangerous files, outdated server software, and version-specific security issues. Written in Perl, it performs comprehensive assessments of web server environments, including CGI scanning and SSL support checks. Nikto's ability to detect a wide range of vulnerabilities makes it a staple tool in web security testing, particularly for legacy systems and environments requiring thorough scrutiny.
githubDescription: Nikto web server scanner

name: Gobuster
description: Directory/file, DNS, and vhost busting tool. Brute-forces URIs, DNS subdomains, virtual host names, and S3 buckets.
longDescription: Gobuster is a popular tool for brute-forcing URIs, DNS subdomains, virtual host names, and S3 buckets. Developed in Go, it is designed for speed and efficiency, allowing users to perform directory/file discovery and DNS enumeration. Gobuster's ability to handle various modes of operation makes it versatile for web security assessments, providing security professionals with a robust solution for uncovering hidden resources and configurations.
githubDescription: Directory/File, DNS and VHost busting tool written in Go

name: Feroxbuster
description: Fast, recursive content discovery tool written in Rust. Like gobuster on steroids with auto-recursion.
longDescription: Feroxbuster is a tool designed to perform forced browsing (directory/file enumeration) and content discovery. It is built in Rust for maximum performance and includes features like automatic recursion, wildcard filtering, output file support, and resume capability. Unlike other brute-forcers, feroxbuster automatically discovers and recurses into new directories as it finds them, building a complete picture of the target's file structure.
useCases: Recursive content discovery and directory bruting
githubDescription: A fast, simple, recursive content discovery tool written in Rust.

name: Sherlock
description: Hunt down social media accounts by username across 400+ social networks simultaneously.
longDescription: Sherlock hunts down social media accounts by username across 400+ social networks simultaneously. Given a username, it queries hundreds of websites to check if an account with that username exists. Results include the profile URLs for any matches found. Sherlock is one of the most popular OSINT tools for username enumeration and is widely used in investigations, background checks, and security assessments.
githubDescription: Hunt down social media accounts by username across social networks

name: Maigret
description: Sherlock fork on steroids. Collects detailed info from 2500+ sites, builds a report with extracted data.
longDescription: Maigret is a powerful OSINT tool designed to collect detailed information from over 2500 websites, building comprehensive reports with the extracted data. As a feature-rich fork of Sherlock, Maigret enhances social media and public data exploration, making it ideal for detailed reconnaissance and investigation tasks. It automates the process of data gathering and reporting, assisting security researchers and investigative professionals in building profiles and uncovering digital footprints.
githubDescription: 🕵️‍♂️ Collect a dossier on a person by username from 3000+ sites

name: theHarvester
description: Gathers emails, names, subdomains, IPs, and URLs from multiple public sources for passive recon.
longDescription: theHarvester is an effective tool for gathering emails, names, subdomains, IPs, and URLs from publicly accessible sources. Written in Python, it is a passive reconnaissance tool that aggregates data from search engines, PGP key servers, and other sources to map and assess potential attack surfaces. Its broad coverage and focus on passive data collection make it a valuable asset for initial reconnaissance phases in penetration testing and security assessments.
githubDescription: E-mails, subdomains and names Harvester - OSINT

name: SpiderFoot
description: Automated OSINT with 200+ modules. Web UI for scanning IPs, domains, emails, names, and more.
longDescription: SpiderFoot automates OSINT collection so you can focus on analysis. With over 200 modules, it queries dozens of data sources to gather intelligence on IP addresses, domain names, email addresses, names, and more. SpiderFoot includes a web-based UI for scan management and result visualization, making it accessible for analysts who prefer a graphical interface. It can also be run from the command line for automation and integration into existing workflows.
useCases: Automated OSINT gathering across multiple data sources
githubDescription: SpiderFoot automates OSINT for threat intelligence and mapping your attack surface.

name: Maltego CE
description: Visual link analysis tool for OSINT. Maps relationships between people, companies, domains, and infrastructure.
longDescription: Maltego CE is a visual link analysis tool that enables comprehensive OSINT investigations by mapping relationships between entities like people, companies, domains, and infrastructure. Developed in Java, it provides a graphical interface to visualize complex data sets using transforms and link analysis. Maltego's ability to reveal hidden connections and patterns makes it indispensable for analysts and investigators conducting in-depth research and threat intelligence operations.

name: Holehe
description: Check if an email is registered on 120+ sites. Uses password recovery mechanisms to verify without logging in.
longDescription: Holehe is a Python-based tool that checks if an email address is registered on over 120 websites by leveraging password recovery mechanisms. It performs passive account discovery without requiring login attempts, providing a non-intrusive way to map email usage across various platforms. Holehe is useful for OSINT practitioners seeking to identify potential account associations and data leakage risks associated with specific email addresses.
githubDescription: holehe allows you to check if the mail is used on different sites like twitter, instagram and will retrieve information on sites with the forgotten password function.

name: Shodan CLI
description: Command-line interface for Shodan, the search engine for internet-connected devices.
longDescription: Shodan CLI is the command-line interface for Shodan, the search engine for internet-connected devices. It allows users to query Shodan's vast database of device information and perform complex searches directly from the terminal. Shodan CLI facilitates network reconnaissance and security assessments by providing detailed data on exposed devices, services, and vulnerabilities, making it a critical tool for cybersecurity researchers and IT professionals.
githubDescription: The official Python library for Shodan

name: Metasploit Framework
description: The world's most used penetration testing framework. Exploit development, payload delivery, post-exploitation.
longDescription: The Metasploit Framework is the world's most widely used penetration testing software. It helps security teams verify vulnerabilities, manage security assessments, and improve security awareness. Metasploit provides a comprehensive environment for writing, testing, and executing exploit code against remote targets. It includes over 2,300 exploits, 1,100 auxiliary modules, 400 post-exploitation modules, and 600 payloads including Meterpreter. The framework supports every phase of a penetration test from reconnaissance to post-exploitation.
useCases: Red team operations and adversary simulation
githubDescription: Metasploit Framework

name: John the Ripper
description: Fast password cracker. Supports hundreds of hash types and ciphers. CPU and GPU modes.
longDescription: John the Ripper is a widely used password cracker designed to perform fast and efficient hashing attacks. Supporting hundreds of hash types and ciphers, it runs in both CPU and GPU modes to maximize performance across different platforms. Known for its versatility and speed, John the Ripper is a staple tool for security professionals conducting password audits and cryptographic research, offering comprehensive support for various attack vectors and techniques.
githubDescription: John the Ripper jumbo - advanced offline password cracker, which supports hundreds of hash and cipher types, and runs on many operating systems, CPUs, GPUs, and even some FPGAs

name: Hashcat
description: World's fastest password recovery tool. GPU-accelerated with support for 300+ hash types.
longDescription: Hashcat is the world's fastest and most advanced password recovery utility, supporting over 300 highly optimized hashing algorithms. It leverages GPU acceleration to crack hashes at unprecedented speeds. Hashcat supports multiple attack modes including dictionary attacks, brute-force, combinator, mask attacks, rule-based attacks, and hybrid attacks. It runs on Linux, Windows, and macOS and supports every major GPU vendor.
useCases: Password policy auditing and compliance testing
Rule-based attack generation for targeted cracking
githubDescription: World's fastest and most advanced password recovery utility

name: Aircrack-ng
description: Complete suite for WiFi network security assessment. Monitoring, attacking, testing, and cracking.
longDescription: Aircrack-ng is a complete suite of tools for assessing WiFi network security. It focuses on monitoring (packet capture and export of data to text files), attacking (replay attacks, deauthentication, fake access points), testing (checking WiFi cards and driver capabilities), and cracking (WEP and WPA PSK). All tools are command-line based, allowing heavy scripting and automation. It works primarily with Linux but also supports Windows, macOS, FreeBSD, and others.

installCommands.from source: git clone https://github.com/aircrack-ng/aircrack-ng && cd aircrack-ng && autoreconf -i && ./configure && make && sudo make install

githubDescription: WiFi security auditing tools suite

name: Volatility 3
description: Advanced memory forensics framework. Extracts artifacts from RAM dumps — processes, network connections, registry.
longDescription: Volatility 3 is the next-generation memory forensics framework. It is a completely rewritten version of the Volatility Framework, designed for speed and reliability. It extracts digital artifacts from volatile memory (RAM) samples, enabling investigators to analyze running processes, network connections, registry keys, loaded modules, and more. It supports Windows, Linux, and macOS memory dumps and is the standard tool for memory forensics in digital investigations and incident response.

installCommands.from source: git clone https://github.com/volatilityfoundation/volatility3.git && cd volatility3 && pip install -e .

githubDescription: Volatility 3.0 development

name: Autopsy
description: Digital forensics platform with GUI. Disk image analysis, timeline analysis, keyword search, hash filtering.
longDescription: Autopsy is a digital forensics platform with a user-friendly GUI, designed to facilitate disk image analysis, timeline analysis, keyword searching, and hash filtering. Developed in Java, it integrates with the Sleuth Kit and supports a wide range of forensic investigations, from file recovery to artifact analysis. Autopsy's comprehensive feature set and ease of use make it a valuable tool for forensic examiners and law enforcement agencies conducting digital investigations.
githubDescription: Autopsy® is a digital forensics platform and graphical interface to The Sleuth Kit® and other digital forensics tools. It can be used by law enforcement, military, and corporate examiners to investigate what happened on a computer. You can even use it to recover photos from your camera's memory card.

name: Wireshark
description: The world's foremost network protocol analyzer. Deep packet inspection for hundreds of protocols.
longDescription: Wireshark is the world's most popular network protocol analyzer. It lets you capture and interactively browse the traffic running on a computer network. It has a rich feature set including deep inspection of hundreds of protocols, live capture and offline analysis, rich VoIP analysis, read/write support for many capture file formats, and powerful display filters. Wireshark is essential for network troubleshooting, security analysis, software development, and education.
githubDescription: Read-only mirror of Wireshark's Git repository at https://gitlab.com/wireshark/wireshark. You're welcome to submit pull requests there.

name: Burp Suite Community
description: Web vulnerability scanner and proxy. Intercept, modify, and replay HTTP/S traffic for web app testing.
longDescription: Burp Suite is the leading toolkit for web application security testing. The Community Edition provides essential manual tools for exploring and testing web applications. It includes an intercepting proxy for viewing and modifying traffic between your browser and target applications, a spider for crawling content and functionality, and tools for analyzing randomness in session tokens. The Professional edition adds an advanced web vulnerability scanner, automated crawling, and extensions via the BApp Store.
githubDescription: Burp Extensions Api

name: sqlmap
description: Automatic SQL injection and database takeover tool. Detects and exploits SQL injection flaws.
longDescription: sqlmap is an automatic SQL injection and database takeover tool written in Python. It detects and exploits SQL injection vulnerabilities across a variety of database management systems, automating the process of vulnerability identification and exploitation. With its extensive set of features, sqlmap facilitates database fingerprinting, data extraction, and even OS-level command execution, making it a must-have tool for penetration testers and security researchers.
githubDescription: Automatic SQL injection and database takeover tool

name: ffuf
description: Fast web fuzzer written in Go. Fuzz anything — URLs, headers, POST data — with blazing speed.
longDescription: ffuf (Fuzz Faster U Fool) is a fast web fuzzer written in Go. It's designed to be versatile, allowing you to fuzz any part of an HTTP request including URLs, headers, POST data, and more. ffuf supports multiple wordlists, custom matchers and filters, recursive scanning, and output in multiple formats. Its speed and flexibility have made it the go-to tool for directory discovery, parameter fuzzing, and virtual host enumeration in bug bounty and penetration testing.
useCases: Directory and file discovery on web servers
githubDescription: Fast web fuzzer written in Go

name: Responder
description: LLMNR/NBT-NS/mDNS poisoner and rogue authentication server. Captures NTLMv1/v2 hashes on the network.
longDescription: Responder is a network tool designed to poison LLMNR, NBT-NS, and mDNS protocols, capturing NTLMv1/v2 hashes from Windows environments. Written in Python, it acts as a rogue authentication server to intercept and capture credentials on the network. Responder's ability to exploit weaknesses in Windows name resolution protocols makes it a powerful tool for security professionals conducting network assessments and Active Directory penetration testing.
githubDescription: Responder is a LLMNR, NBT-NS and MDNS poisoner, with built-in HTTP/SMB/MSSQL/FTP/LDAP rogue authentication server supporting NTLMv1/NTLMv2/LMv2, Extended Security NTLMSSP and Basic HTTP authentication.

name: BloodHound
description: Active Directory attack path mapping. Visualizes privilege escalation paths using graph theory.
longDescription: BloodHound uses graph theory to reveal hidden and often unintended relationships within an Active Directory or Azure environment. Attackers use BloodHound to identify complex attack paths that would otherwise be impossible to identify. Defenders use it to identify and eliminate those same attack paths. Both red and blue teams can use BloodHound to gain a deeper understanding of privilege relationships in Active Directory environments.
useCases: Security posture assessment for AD environments
Blue team hardening of AD permissions
githubDescription: Six Degrees of Domain Admin

name: Impacket
description: Collection of Python classes for working with network protocols. Essential for Windows/AD pentesting.
longDescription: Impacket is a collection of Python classes for working with network protocols, essential for Windows and Active Directory pentesting. It provides low-level programmatic access to protocols like SMB, RDP, and LDAP, enabling the development of custom network tools and exploitation scripts. Impacket's extensive support for protocol manipulation makes it a critical resource for security researchers and penetration testers targeting Windows-based environments.
githubDescription: Impacket is a collection of Python classes for working with network protocols.

name: Ghidra
description: NSA's reverse engineering framework. Disassembly, decompilation, graphing, and scripting for binary analysis.
longDescription: Ghidra is a software reverse engineering (SRE) framework created and maintained by the National Security Agency Research Directorate. It includes a full-featured suite of high-end software analysis tools for analyzing compiled code across a variety of platforms. Capabilities include disassembly, assembly, decompilation, graphing, and scripting, along with hundreds of other features. Ghidra supports a wide range of processor instruction sets and executable formats, and users can extend it through plugins and scripts written in Java or Python.
useCases: Binary reverse engineering and malware analysis
Firmware analysis and embedded systems research
githubDescription: Ghidra is a software reverse engineering (SRE) framework

name: ExifTool
description: Read, write, and edit metadata in files. Supports EXIF, GPS, IPTC, XMP, and more across dozens of formats.
longDescription: ExifTool is a platform-independent Perl library plus a command-line application for reading, writing, and editing metadata in a wide variety of file types. It supports a comprehensive range of metadata formats including EXIF, GPS, IPTC, and XMP, across multiple file formats such as JPEG, PNG, PDF, and more. ExifTool is notable for its ability to handle batch processing and its extensive range of supported metadata types, making it a versatile tool for digital forensics and media management.
githubDescription: ExifTool meta information reader/writer

name: GoPhish
description: Open-source phishing framework. Create campaigns, track results, and train users with realistic simulations.
longDescription: GoPhish is an open-source phishing framework that makes it easy to test your organization's exposure to phishing. It provides a web-based interface for creating phishing campaigns, tracking results in real-time, and generating reports. GoPhish supports email templates, landing pages, and sending profiles. It's designed for authorized phishing simulations and security awareness training, not malicious use.
useCases: Phishing simulation and security awareness training
githubDescription: Open-Source Phishing Toolkit

name: PhoneInfoga
description: Advanced phone number OSINT. Scans phone numbers using free resources to gather standard and disposable info.
longDescription: PhoneInfoga is an advanced tool for performing OSINT on phone numbers. It can scan phone numbers using free resources to gather detailed information such as carrier details, geolocation data, and whether the number is disposable. Written in Go, it leverages various online APIs without requiring direct interaction with the phone number, making it a useful tool for security researchers and investigators looking to uncover information with minimal trace.
githubDescription: Information gathering framework for phone numbers

name: Recon-ng
description: Full-featured reconnaissance framework. Modular design with a Metasploit-like interface for OSINT gathering.
longDescription: Recon-ng is a web reconnaissance framework with a modular design and a Metasploit-like interface. It facilitates the gathering of open-source intelligence (OSINT) by incorporating a wide range of modules that perform tasks like data collection, analysis, and reporting. Recon-ng supports API key management, integrates with various third-party services, and provides a powerful scripting environment for customizing reconnaissance workflows, making it a staple in the toolkit of security professionals.
githubDescription: Open Source Intelligence gathering tool aimed at reducing the time spent harvesting information from open sources.

name: Katana
description: Next-gen crawling and spidering framework. Headless browser and standard mode with automatic form fill.
longDescription: Katana is a next-generation web crawling and spidering framework designed for efficient data gathering. It operates in both headless browser and standard modes, enabling it to automatically fill forms and navigate complex web structures. Developed in Go, Katana is optimized for performance and flexibility, making it suitable for both simple and advanced web reconnaissance tasks. Its ability to handle dynamic content makes it a valuable asset for penetration testers and researchers.
githubDescription: A next-generation crawling and spidering framework.

name: dnsx
description: Fast multi-purpose DNS toolkit. Retries, wildcard filtering, and multiple resolver support.
longDescription: dnsx is a high-performance DNS toolkit designed for various DNS reconnaissance tasks. It supports multiple DNS resolvers and incorporates features like retries and wildcard filtering to enhance accuracy. Written in Go, dnsx is optimized for speed and reliability, making it suitable for large-scale DNS enumeration and analysis. Its versatility and ease of use make it a popular choice among security researchers and network administrators.
githubDescription: dnsx is a fast and multi-purpose DNS toolkit allow to run multiple DNS queries of your choice with a list of user-supplied resolvers.

name: Knock
description: Subdomain enumeration tool using wordlist and DNS resolution. Supports VirusTotal and zone transfers.
longDescription: Knock is a Python-based subdomain enumeration tool that utilizes wordlists and DNS resolution techniques. It supports integration with VirusTotal for enhanced discovery and can perform zone transfers when possible. Knock's ability to leverage both wordlists and DNS records makes it a robust tool for discovering subdomains, providing valuable insights during penetration testing and security assessments.
githubDescription: Knock Subdomain Scan

name: Assetfinder

longDescription: Assetfinder is a tool designed to discover domains and subdomains that are potentially related to a given root domain. It aggregates data from multiple passive sources, including DNS records and public datasets, to identify subdomains without direct interaction with the target. Written in Go, Assetfinder is known for its speed and efficiency, making it an essential tool for reconnaissance efforts in security testing and research.
githubDescription: Find domains and subdomains related to a given domain

name: waybackurls

longDescription: waybackurls is a tool that retrieves all known URLs for a domain from the Wayback Machine. It is particularly useful for uncovering hidden or forgotten endpoints, providing insights into the historical structure and content of a website. Written in Go, waybackurls is efficient and easy to use, making it a valuable resource for web application security testing and digital forensics.
githubDescription: Fetch all the URLs that the Wayback Machine knows about for a domain

name: gau
description: Get All URLs. Fetches known URLs from AlienVault OTX, Wayback Machine, Common Crawl, and URLScan.
longDescription: gau (Get All URLs) is a tool designed to fetch known URLs from multiple sources like AlienVault OTX, the Wayback Machine, Common Crawl, and URLScan. It provides a comprehensive view of a domain's URL footprint by aggregating historical and current data from these services. Written in Go, gau is optimized for speed and is a popular choice for security researchers looking to identify potential attack vectors in web applications.
githubDescription: Fetch known URLs from AlienVault's Open Threat Exchange, the Wayback Machine, and Common Crawl.

name: Arjun

longDescription: Arjun is an HTTP parameter discovery suite that identifies hidden query parameters in web applications using smart heuristics. It automates the process of parameter discovery, helping security researchers uncover potential attack vectors that might otherwise be missed. Written in Python, Arjun is designed for efficiency and accuracy, making it a valuable tool for web application security assessments.
githubDescription: HTTP parameter discovery suite.

name: WPScan
description: WordPress security scanner. Enumerates plugins, themes, users, and checks for known vulnerabilities.
longDescription: WPScan is a WordPress security scanner that identifies vulnerabilities by enumerating plugins, themes, and users. It checks for known vulnerabilities in the WordPress core and its components using a regularly updated database. Written in Ruby, WPScan is widely used by security professionals to assess the security posture of WordPress installations and is an essential tool for anyone managing or auditing WordPress sites.
githubDescription: WPScan WordPress security scanner. Written for security professionals and blog maintainers to test the security of their WordPress websites. Contact us via contact@wpscan.com

name: OpenVAS

longDescription: OpenVAS is a comprehensive vulnerability scanner that offers over 50,000 Network Vulnerability Tests (NVTs). longDescription: XSStrike is an advanced cross-site scripting (XSS) detection suite that includes a powerful fuzzing engine, context analysis, and WAF detection/bypass capabilities. longDescription: Commix is an automated tool for testing web applications for command injection vulnerabilities. longDescription: Social Analyzer is a versatile tool for finding and analyzing profiles across over 1000 social media platforms. githubDescription: API, CLI, and Web App for analyzing and finding a person's profile in 1000 social media \ websites

name: Twint

longDescription: Twint is an advanced Twitter scraping tool that bypasses the need for API keys, enabling users to collect data such as tweets, followers, and favorites. githubDescription: An advanced Twitter scraping & OSINT tool written in Python that doesn't use Twitter's API, allowing you to scrape a user's followers, following, Tweets and more while evading most API limitations.

name: Instaloader

longDescription: Instaloader is a Python-based tool for downloading Instagram content, including photos, videos, stories, and metadata. Instaloader can operate without user interaction, making it suitable for both automated data collection and manual investigations. name: Osintgram

longDescription: Osintgram is an OSINT tool designed for collecting data from Instagram profiles. It extracts information such as emails, phone numbers, user details, followers, and geolocation data, facilitating targeted investigations. githubDescription: Osintgram is a OSINT tool on Instagram. It offers an interactive shell to perform analysis on Instagram account of any users by its nickname

name: TweetFeed
description: Real-time feed of IOCs shared on Twitter by the infosec community. Domains, IPs, URLs, SHA256 hashes.
longDescription: TweetFeed provides a real-time stream of Indicators of Compromise (IOCs) shared by the infosec community on Twitter. It captures domains, IPs, URLs, and SHA256 hashes, serving as a dynamic threat intelligence source. Built with Python, TweetFeed continuously monitors Twitter for the latest security insights, making it an essential tool for cybersecurity professionals seeking up-to-date threat information.

longDescription: Elliptic Lens is a crypto compliance and investigation tool designed for cross-chain tracing, sanctions screening, and risk profiling. It allows users to trace funds across different blockchains and assess potential regulatory risks. Elliptic Lens is a key resource for compliance teams aiming to ensure adherence to global regulations and mitigate financial crime in the crypto space.

name: Transpose
description: SQL-based blockchain data API. Query decoded transactions, token transfers, NFT sales, and DeFi events.
longDescription: Transpose provides a SQL-based blockchain data API, allowing users to query decoded transactions, token transfers, NFT sales, and DeFi events. It simplifies access to complex blockchain data with SQL queries, making it accessible for developers and analysts. Transpose's approach enables detailed insights and data-driven decision-making in crypto applications.
githubDescription: Python SDK wrapper for the Transpose API suite

name: evm-trace
description: Python library for parsing and analyzing EVM call traces. Decode internal transactions and visualize call trees.
longDescription: Evm-trace is a Python library for parsing and analyzing Ethereum Virtual Machine (EVM) call traces, enabling users to decode internal transactions and visualize call trees. It is essential for developers and auditors requiring detailed insights into smart contract execution. Evm-trace aids in debugging and optimizing smart contracts by providing a clear view of execution paths and state changes.
githubDescription: Ethereum Virtual Machine transaction tracing tool

name: ChainSight
description: Real-time monitoring of DEX trades, bridge transfers, and large movements. Custom alerts via webhook or Telegram.
longDescription: ChainSight offers real-time monitoring of DEX trades, bridge transfers, and large crypto movements, with custom alerting via webhook or Telegram. It provides immediate insights into significant blockchain events, aiding in prompt decision-making and risk management. ChainSight's capabilities make it a vital tool for traders and analysts monitoring dynamic crypto markets.

name: Bitcoin Core CLI
description: Official Bitcoin node with full RPC interface. Query the blockchain directly — raw transactions, mempool, UTXO lookups.
longDescription: Bitcoin Core CLI is the official command-line interface for interacting with a Bitcoin node, offering a full RPC interface to query blockchain data, including raw transactions, mempool, and UTXO lookups. It provides deep access to Bitcoin's blockchain, supporting various operations for developers and administrators running full nodes. This tool is a cornerstone for those requiring comprehensive blockchain data access in Bitcoin networks.
githubDescription: Bitcoin Core integration/staging tree

name: Geth (Go Ethereum)
description: Official Go implementation of Ethereum. Full node, archive node, and light client with rich JSON-RPC API.
longDescription: Geth (Go Ethereum) is the official Go implementation of the Ethereum protocol, capable of running as a full node, archive node, or light client. It offers a rich JSON-RPC API for interacting with the Ethereum blockchain, supporting a wide range of applications and services. As a core component of the Ethereum network, Geth is integral for developers and operators maintaining blockchain infrastructure.
githubDescription: Go implementation of the Ethereum protocol

name: Solana CLI
description: Solana blockchain tools. Query accounts, transactions, programs, and stake info. Built-in token management.
longDescription: Solana CLI is a suite of command-line tools for interacting with the Solana blockchain, enabling users to query accounts, transactions, programs, and stake information. It includes built-in functionalities for token management, supporting developers and users in navigating Solana's blockchain. The CLI's comprehensive capabilities make it a fundamental tool for those developing or managing Solana-based applications.
githubDescription: Web-Scale Blockchain for fast, secure, scalable, decentralized apps and marketplaces.

name: Drainer Detector
description: Detect wallet drainer contracts and phishing approval signatures. Scans for SetApprovalForAll and Permit2 abuse.
longDescription: Drainer Detector is a Python-based tool designed to identify and flag wallet drainer contracts and phishing approval signatures in the crypto space. It specifically scans for the misuse of SetApprovalForAll and Permit2 functions, which are often exploited in phishing attacks. The tool helps protect users by detecting potential vulnerabilities in smart contracts and alerting them to possible security risks before they approve any transactions.

name: mev-inspect-py
description: Detect and analyze MEV (Maximal Extractable Value) on Ethereum. Finds arbitrage, liquidations, and sandwiches.
longDescription: mev-inspect-py is a Python tool for detecting and analyzing Maximal Extractable Value (MEV) on the Ethereum blockchain. It identifies and categorizes various MEV opportunities such as arbitrage, liquidations, and sandwich attacks. By analyzing Ethereum transactions, mev-inspect-py provides insights into how these strategies are executed, helping researchers and developers understand the impact of MEV on the network.
githubDescription: 🔎 an MEV inspector for Ethereum 🔎

name: EigenPhi Tools
description: DeFi attack and MEV analysis. Visualizes flash loan attacks, rug pulls, and arbitrage with transaction decoding.
longDescription: EigenPhi Tools offer a comprehensive analysis suite for DeFi attacks and MEV events, with a focus on visualizing complex transactions such as flash loan attacks, rug pulls, and arbitrage opportunities. It decodes blockchain transactions to provide a clear picture of attack vectors and profit extraction methods. This Python-based tool aids in understanding the dynamics of DeFi and the risks associated with various financial operations.

name: Token Sniffer
description: Automated scam token detection. Analyzes contract code for honeypots, hidden mints, tax manipulation, and rug vectors.
longDescription: Token Sniffer is an automated tool written in TypeScript that detects scam tokens on the blockchain by analyzing smart contract code. It looks for common fraudulent practices such as honeypots, hidden mints, tax manipulation, and rug pull vectors. This tool is essential for investors and developers looking to identify and avoid deceptive tokens before they cause financial loss.

name: RugDoc Scanner
description: Smart contract risk assessment. Reviews DeFi contracts for common rug pull patterns and backdoor functions.
longDescription: RugDoc Scanner is a Python-based tool for assessing the risk of smart contracts, particularly in the DeFi space. It reviews code for patterns commonly associated with rug pulls and backdoor functionalities. By analyzing contract code, RugDoc Scanner helps users identify potential security threats and avoid malicious projects that could lead to significant financial losses.

name: Cryo
description: Extract blockchain data into Parquet or CSV. High-performance bulk exporter for blocks, transactions, logs, and traces.
longDescription: Cryo is a high-performance blockchain data extraction tool built by Paradigm. It extracts blockchain data into Parquet or CSV format, making it easy to analyze with tools like DuckDB, Pandas, or Polars. Cryo supports extracting blocks, transactions, logs, traces, state diffs, and more from any EVM chain. It's designed for researchers and analysts who need to work with large volumes of on-chain data efficiently.
useCases: Bulk extraction of blockchain data for analysis
Building local datasets for forensic investigation
githubDescription: cryo is the easiest way to extract blockchain data to parquet, csv, json, or python dataframes

name: Reth
description: High-performance Ethereum execution client in Rust. Blazing fast sync, archive mode, and modular architecture.
longDescription: Reth is a high-performance Ethereum execution client developed in Rust, designed to provide efficient blockchain synchronization and execution. Its modular architecture allows for rapid deployment and customization, while its archive mode supports comprehensive data retrieval. Reth is notable for its speed and reliability, making it a valuable asset for developers and operators of Ethereum nodes.
githubDescription: Modular, contributor-friendly and blazing-fast implementation of the Ethereum protocol, in Rust

name: Cast (Foundry)
description: Swiss army knife for Ethereum. Send transactions, query chain data, decode calldata, compute hashes — all from CLI.
longDescription: Cast is a command-line tool from the Foundry suite that acts as a Swiss army knife for Ethereum. It can send transactions, query on-chain data, decode calldata and ABI-encode function calls, convert between data formats, compute hashes, resolve ENS names, and much more. Cast is essential for blockchain investigators who need to quickly decode transactions, look up contract storage, or trace calls without writing code.
useCases: Decoding transaction calldata and event logs
Quick blockchain queries from the command line
githubDescription: Foundry is a blazing fast, portable and modular toolkit for Ethereum application development written in Rust.

name: Wfuzz
description: Web application fuzzer. Brute force parameters, directories, headers, and authentication credentials.
longDescription: Wfuzz is a Python-based web application fuzzing tool used to brute force web parameters, directories, headers, and authentication credentials. It provides a flexible framework for security testing, allowing users to customize their attack vectors and payloads. Wfuzz is widely used for its ability to handle complex web application testing scenarios with ease and precision.
githubDescription: Web application fuzzer

name: WhatWeb
description: Web technology fingerprinter. Identifies CMS, frameworks, JS libraries, servers, and analytics from HTTP responses.
longDescription: WhatWeb is a Ruby-based web technology fingerprinting tool that identifies various components of a website, such as CMS, frameworks, JavaScript libraries, servers, and analytics platforms. By analyzing HTTP responses, WhatWeb provides detailed information on the technologies used by a target website. It is a valuable tool for reconnaissance and vulnerability assessment in web security testing.
githubDescription: Next generation web scanner

name: Subjack
description: Subdomain takeover tool. Checks if CNAME records point to deprovisioned cloud services.
longDescription: Subjack is a Go-based tool for detecting subdomain takeover vulnerabilities by checking if CNAME records point to deprovisioned cloud services. It identifies potential security risks where attackers could hijack subdomains to serve malicious content. Subjack is an essential tool for organizations looking to secure their DNS infrastructure against such vulnerabilities.
githubDescription: DNS Takeover tool written in Go

name: Altdns
description: Subdomain discovery through alterations and permutations. Generates mutated wordlists from known subdomains.
longDescription: Altdns is a Python tool that enhances subdomain discovery by generating permutations and mutations of known subdomains to uncover additional DNS entries. It creates comprehensive wordlists that can be used in conjunction with other DNS enumeration tools to expand the scope of domain reconnaissance. Altdns is effective in increasing the coverage of subdomain discovery efforts.
githubDescription: Generates permutations, alterations and mutations of subdomains and then resolves them

name: TruffleHog
description: Find leaked credentials in Git repos, S3 buckets, and filesystems. Regex and entropy-based detection.
longDescription: TruffleHog scans for leaked credentials and secrets in Git repositories, S3 buckets, filesystems, and more. It uses both regex patterns and entropy analysis to detect API keys, passwords, tokens, and other sensitive data that may have been accidentally committed. TruffleHog supports over 800 credential detectors and can verify discovered credentials against the actual services to confirm they are still active.

installCommands.from source: git clone https://github.com/trufflesecurity/trufflehog.git && cd trufflehog && go install

useCases: Scanning Git history for leaked secrets
githubDescription: Find, verify, and analyze leaked credentials

name: Gitleaks
description: Scan Git repos for hardcoded secrets like passwords, API keys, and tokens. CI/CD ready.
longDescription: Gitleaks is a Go-based tool for scanning Git repositories to identify hardcoded secrets, such as passwords, API keys, and tokens. It integrates seamlessly into CI/CD pipelines, providing continuous monitoring and alerting for potential security leaks. Gitleaks helps developers maintain secure codebases by preventing the accidental exposure of sensitive information.
githubDescription: Find secrets with Gitleaks 🔑

name: DNSRecon
description: DNS enumeration script. Zone transfers, reverse lookups, Google dorking, SRV records, and DNSSEC testing.
longDescription: DNSRecon is a Python script designed for DNS enumeration, offering capabilities such as zone transfers, reverse lookups, Google dorking, SRV record discovery, and DNSSEC testing. It provides a comprehensive approach to mapping an organization's DNS infrastructure. DNSRecon is widely used by penetration testers and security researchers for its thoroughness in DNS analysis.
githubDescription: DNS Enumeration Script

name: TheFatRat
description: Exploit and payload generator. Creates backdoors with msfvenom, compiles with anti-AV evasion techniques.
longDescription: TheFatRat is a shell-based tool for generating exploits and payloads, utilizing msfvenom for creating backdoors with advanced anti-AV evasion techniques. It compiles payloads to bypass common antivirus detections, making it a popular choice for penetration testers seeking to test the robustness of target systems. TheFatRat offers a wide range of payload customization options.
githubDescription: Thefatrat a massive exploiting tool : Easy tool to generate backdoor and easy tool to post exploitation attack like browser attack and etc . This tool compiles a malware with popular payload and then the compiled malware can be execute on windows, android, mac . The malware that created with this tool also have an ability to bypass most AV software protection .

name: SecLists
description: The security tester's companion. Huge collection of wordlists — usernames, passwords, URLs, fuzzing payloads, shells.
longDescription: SecLists is an extensive collection of wordlists used by security testers for various applications, including usernames, passwords, URLs, fuzzing payloads, and web shells. This shell-based repository is indispensable for penetration testers and security researchers who require comprehensive lists for brute force attacks, vulnerability assessments, and other security testing tasks.
githubDescription: SecLists is the security tester's companion. It's a collection of multiple types of lists used during security assessments, collected in one place. List types include usernames, passwords, URLs, sensitive data patterns, fuzzing payloads, web shells, and many more.

name: Mimikatz
description: Extract plaintext passwords, hashes, PIN codes, and Kerberos tickets from Windows memory.
longDescription: Mimikatz is a C-based tool that extracts plaintext passwords, hashes, PIN codes, and Kerberos tickets from Windows memory, particularly from the LSASS process. It is widely used for credential dumping and privilege escalation in Windows environments. Mimikatz is an essential tool for penetration testers and security professionals aiming to assess security vulnerabilities in Windows systems.
githubDescription: A little tool to play with Windows security

name: LaZagne
description: Password recovery tool. Retrieves stored passwords from browsers, WiFi, databases, sysadmin tools, and more.
longDescription: LaZagne is a password recovery tool designed to retrieve stored credentials from various applications. Written in Python, it targets browsers, WiFi networks, databases, and system administration tools, among others. LaZagne operates by accessing application-specific memory spaces and files to extract sensitive data. It is frequently used by penetration testers and security professionals for credential recovery in forensic investigations.
githubDescription: Credentials recovery project

name: enum4linux-ng
description: Windows/Samba enumeration tool. Extracts user lists, share info, group policies, and OS details via SMB.
longDescription: enum4linux-ng is a Python-based tool for enumerating information from Windows and Samba systems. It extracts user lists, share information, group policies, and operating system details by leveraging the Server Message Block (SMB) protocol. Designed as an update to the original enum4linux, it offers improved performance and additional features. It is a valuable asset for security researchers conducting network reconnaissance and vulnerability assessments.
githubDescription: A next generation version of enum4linux (a Windows/Samba enumeration tool) with additional features like JSON/YAML export. Aimed for security professionals and CTF players.

name: GoBuster DNS
description: DNS subdomain bruting using Go. Concurrent queries with wildcard detection and custom resolvers.
longDescription: GoBuster DNS is a subdomain brute-forcing tool built in Go, known for its speed and concurrency. It uses wordlists to perform DNS queries, detecting subdomains while incorporating wildcard detection and allowing custom DNS resolvers. Ideal for penetration testers, it supports high-performance, stealthy enumeration of subdomains, providing insights into potential attack surfaces. Its design focuses on efficiency and ease of use in large-scale assessments.
githubDescription: Directory/File, DNS and VHost busting tool written in Go

name: ProxyChains-ng
description: Force any TCP connection through SOCKS4/5 or HTTP proxies. Chain multiple proxies for anonymity.
longDescription: ProxyChains-ng is a preloader which hooks calls to sockets in dynamically linked programs and redirects them through one or more SOCKS/HTTP proxies. It allows you to use SSH, Telnet, wget, and virtually any TCP application through a proxy chain. It supports SOCKS4, SOCKS5, and HTTP CONNECT proxy servers and allows mixing different proxy types in the same chain. Widely used for pivoting through compromised hosts and anonymous scanning.
useCases: Routing tools through SOCKS proxies for anonymity
Pivoting through compromised hosts during pentests
Chaining multiple proxies for operational security
Forcing non-proxy-aware tools through proxy tunnels
githubDescription: proxychains ng (new generation) - a preloader which hooks calls to sockets in dynamically linked programs and redirects it through one or more socks/http proxies. continuation of the unmaintained proxychains project. the sf.net page is currently not updated, use releases from github release page instead.

name: Socat
description: Multipurpose relay tool. Bidirectional data transfer between two data channels — sockets, files, pipes, devices.
longDescription: Socat (SOcket CAT) is a command-line utility that establishes two bidirectional byte streams and transfers data between them. It's like an extended version of netcat with support for SSL, IPv6, SOCKS4 proxying, and dozens of address types. Socat can create virtual serial connections, tunnel traffic through SSL, act as a simple TCP forwarder, and much more. It's a sysadmin essential that doubles as a powerful offensive tool for port forwarding, bind/reverse shells, and encrypted tunnels.
githubDescription: Mirror of the socat source code with pre-built releases for Linux (x64 and x86), Windows (x64 and x86), and MacOS (x64)

name: ngrok
description: Expose local servers to the internet via secure tunnels. Instant public URLs for localhost services.
longDescription: ngrok creates secure tunnels from a public endpoint to a locally running service. It captures all traffic for inspection, replay, and analysis. While designed for legitimate development and testing workflows, ngrok is commonly used in red team operations to exfiltrate data, establish reverse tunnels to C2 infrastructure, and bypass NAT/firewall restrictions. The free tier provides random subdomains; paid plans offer custom domains and IP restrictions.
useCases: Exposing local dev servers for webhook testing
Creating reverse tunnels for C2 callbacks
Traffic inspection and replay during development
Quick file sharing and data exfiltration
githubDescription: Unified ingress for developers

name: Rclone
description: rsync for cloud storage. Sync, copy, and mount 70+ cloud providers. Command-line Swiss army knife for cloud data.
longDescription: Rclone is a command-line program to manage files on cloud storage. It supports over 70 cloud storage providers including S3, Google Drive, Dropbox, OneDrive, Azure Blob, and more. While it's a legitimate and extremely useful sysadmin tool, it's frequently observed in ransomware operations and data theft incidents for large-scale exfiltration to attacker-controlled cloud storage. DFIR teams track rclone usage as a key indicator of compromise.
useCases: Backup automation to multiple cloud providers
Known exfiltration tool in ransomware operations
Mounting cloud storage as local filesystem
githubDescription: "rsync for cloud storage" - Google Drive, S3, Dropbox, Backblaze B2, One Drive, Swift, Hubic, Wasabi, Google Cloud Storage, Azure Blob, Azure Files, Yandex Files

name: GTFOBins
description: Curated list of Unix binaries that can be used to bypass security restrictions. Living off the land, documented.
longDescription: GTFOBins is a curated list of Unix binaries that can be exploited by an attacker to bypass local security restrictions on misconfigured systems. It documents how standard system binaries (find, vim, python, bash, etc.) can be abused for privilege escalation, file read/write, shell escape, reverse shell creation, and more. It's the definitive reference for Living Off The Land techniques on Linux/Unix systems. Every pentester and red teamer has this bookmarked.
useCases: Linux privilege escalation via standard binaries
Reverse shell creation using system tools
Security hardening — knowing what to restrict
githubDescription: GTFOBins is a curated list of Unix-like executables that can be used to bypass local security restrictions in misconfigured systems.

name: LOLBAS
description: Living Off The Land Binaries, Scripts and Libraries for Windows. Documenting every Windows binary with offensive potential.
longDescription: LOLBAS (Living Off The Land Binaries, Scripts and Libraries) is the Windows equivalent of GTFOBins. It documents Windows binaries, scripts, and libraries that can be used for file download, code execution, lateral movement, persistence, credential theft, and more — all using tools already present on the system. This includes certutil, mshta, rundll32, regsvr32, bitsadmin, and dozens more. Understanding LOLBAS is essential for both red teams (evasion) and blue teams (detection).
useCases: File download via certutil, bitsadmin, etc.
Code execution through mshta, rundll32, regsvr32
githubDescription: Living Off The Land Binaries And Scripts - (LOLBins and LOLScripts)

name: Sysinternals Suite
description: Microsoft's advanced system utilities. PsExec, Process Monitor, Autoruns, TCPView — essential for both ops and offense.
longDescription: Sysinternals is a suite of advanced system utilities by Microsoft, used daily by system administrators worldwide. However, tools like PsExec (remote command execution), ProcDump (memory dumping), and Autoruns (persistence detection/creation) are heavily used in offensive operations. PsExec in particular is one of the most common lateral movement tools observed in real-world intrusions. Attackers use legitimate, Microsoft-signed binaries to blend in with normal administrative activity.
useCases: Process memory dumping with ProcDump (credential extraction)
Persistence discovery and creation via Autoruns
githubDescription: Content for sysinternals.com

name: Empire
description: Post-exploitation agent built on PowerShell and Python. Cryptologically-secure communications and flexible architecture.
longDescription: Empire is a post-exploitation and adversary emulation framework that uses PowerShell (Windows) and Python (Linux/macOS) agents. It features cryptologically-secure communications, a flexible architecture, and a wide range of post-exploitation modules. Empire was originally developed by BC Security and has been used extensively in both red team operations and real-world attacks. It supports credential harvesting, lateral movement, persistence, privilege escalation, and data exfiltration.
useCases: Post-exploitation framework for red team engagements
githubDescription: Empire is a post-exploitation and adversary emulation framework that is used to aid Red Teams and Penetration Testers.

name: Tor
description: Anonymous communication network. Routes traffic through multiple relays to conceal location and usage.
longDescription: Tor (The Onion Router) is free software and an open network that helps defend against traffic analysis. It directs Internet traffic through a worldwide overlay network of relays to conceal a user's location and usage from surveillance and traffic analysis. Tor is used by journalists, activists, whistleblowers, and privacy-conscious individuals — but also by threat actors for anonymous operations, C2 infrastructure, and accessing dark web markets. Understanding Tor is essential for both operational security and investigating dark web activity.
githubDescription: unofficial git repo -- report bugs/issues/pull requests on https://gitlab.torproject.org/ --

name: OpenSSH
description: The SSH protocol implementation. Remote access, tunneling, port forwarding, SOCKS proxying — everywhere.
longDescription: OpenSSH is the premier connectivity tool for remote login using the SSH protocol. While it's the most fundamental sysadmin tool in existence, its tunneling capabilities make it equally powerful offensively. SSH supports local/remote/dynamic port forwarding, SOCKS proxying, and can create encrypted tunnels that bypass firewall restrictions. Attackers use SSH for pivoting, data exfiltration over encrypted channels, and maintaining persistent access to compromised systems.
useCases: Local and remote port forwarding for pivoting
githubDescription: Portable OpenSSH

name: NetExec
description: Network execution tool — the maintained successor to CrackMapExec. SMB, LDAP, WinRM, SSH, MSSQL, and more.
longDescription: NetExec (nxc) is the actively maintained successor to CrackMapExec. It's a network service exploitation tool that supports SMB, LDAP, WinRM, SSH, MSSQL, RDP, and more. NetExec automates credential testing, command execution, and enumeration across network services. It's indispensable for Active Directory pentesting — spray credentials, dump SAM/LSA, execute commands, and enumerate shares across hundreds of hosts in seconds.
useCases: SMB share enumeration and access testing
githubDescription: The Network Execution Tool

name: CobaltStrike Parser
description: Parse and extract configs from Cobalt Strike beacons. Identify C2 servers, watermarks, and malleable C2 profiles.
useCases: Incident response triage for CS infections
longDescription: CobaltStrike Parser is a Python tool used to extract configurations from Cobalt Strike beacon payloads. It identifies crucial elements such as command and control (C2) server addresses, watermarks, and malleable C2 profiles. This tool aids analysts and incident responders in dissecting malicious payloads, enabling them to understand threat actor infrastructure and communication patterns. It's essential for digital forensics and incident response (DFIR) operations.

name: PowerSploit
description: Collection of PowerShell post-exploitation modules. Credential theft, privilege escalation, persistence, exfiltration.
longDescription: PowerSploit is a collection of Microsoft PowerShell modules that aid penetration testers during all phases of an assessment. Modules include PowerView (AD enumeration), Invoke-Mimikatz (in-memory credential extraction), Get-GPPPassword (Group Policy preference passwords), and dozens more. While the project is archived, it remains one of the most referenced PowerShell offensive toolkits and its techniques are still actively used.
githubDescription: PowerSploit - A PowerShell Post-Exploitation Framework

name: Nishang
description: PowerShell offensive security framework. Shells, backdoors, information gathering, and privilege escalation.
longDescription: Nishang is a comprehensive offensive security framework composed of PowerShell scripts. It provides tools for executing shells, creating backdoors, gathering system information, and performing privilege escalation. Nishang is widely utilized in penetration testing and red team engagements due to its versatility and ability to exploit Windows environments. It serves both as an educational resource and a practical toolkit for security professionals.
githubDescription: Nishang - Offensive PowerShell for red team, penetration testing and offensive security.

name: dnscat2
description: Create an encrypted C2 channel over DNS. Tunnel data through DNS queries when all other protocols are blocked.
longDescription: dnscat2 creates an encrypted command-and-control channel over the DNS protocol. It's designed for situations where all other outbound traffic is blocked but DNS resolution is still permitted — which is almost always. The tool consists of a client that runs on compromised hosts and a server that runs on an authoritative DNS server controlled by the attacker. All communication is encrypted and tunneled through DNS TXT, CNAME, or MX records.
useCases: C2 communication over DNS when other protocols are blocked
Bypassing firewall restrictions that allow DNS
Encrypted tunneling through corporate DNS infrastructure

name: Mythic
description: Collaborative, multi-platform C2 framework. Docker-based with web UI, multiple agent types, and plugin architecture.
longDescription: Mythic is a multiplayer, command and control platform for red team operations. It's designed to be collaborative, allowing multiple operators to manage agents simultaneously through a web-based UI. Mythic supports multiple agent types (Apollo for .NET, Poseidon for Go, Medusa for Python, etc.) and uses a plugin architecture for extensibility. All communication is containerized and managed through Docker. It tracks operations, manages credentials, and provides file management — a complete red team platform.
useCases: Full red team C2 operations with web UI
Custom agent development via plugin system
githubDescription: A collaborative, multi-platform, red teaming framework

name: Havoc
description: Modern C2 framework. Qt-based GUI, BOF support, custom agents, and a Cobalt Strike-inspired workflow.
longDescription: Havoc is a modern, malleable post-exploitation command and control framework. It features a cross-platform Qt-based GUI, support for Beacon Object Files (BOFs), custom agent development through its Agent SDK, and encrypted C2 communication. Havoc was designed as an open-source alternative to Cobalt Strike with a similar operator experience. It supports multiple listeners, team servers, and has a growing library of post-exploitation modules.
useCases: Red team operations with GUI-based C2
githubDescription: The Havoc Framework

name: Rubeus
description: C# toolset for raw Kerberos interaction and abuse. AS-REP roasting, Kerberoasting, ticket manipulation, delegation attacks.
longDescription: Rubeus is a C# toolset for raw Kerberos interaction and abuses. It's the go-to tool for Kerberos-based attacks in Active Directory environments. Rubeus supports AS-REP roasting, Kerberoasting, unconstrained/constrained/resource-based constrained delegation abuse, S4U ticket requests, ticket extraction and renewal, and golden/silver ticket creation. It can be loaded reflectively in memory, making it a staple of modern AD attack chains.
useCases: Kerberoasting to extract service account hashes
AS-REP roasting against accounts without pre-auth
Kerberos delegation abuse for privilege escalation
githubDescription: Trying to tame the three-headed dog.

name: Certipy
description: Active Directory Certificate Services (AD CS) abuse tool. Find and exploit certificate template misconfigurations.
longDescription: Certipy is a Python tool for enumerating and abusing Active Directory Certificate Services (AD CS). It can identify vulnerable certificate templates, request certificates for privilege escalation, perform ESC1-ESC8 attacks, extract CA private keys, and forge certificates. AD CS misconfigurations are among the most impactful attack paths in modern Active Directory environments, and Certipy automates the entire attack chain from enumeration to exploitation.
githubDescription: Tool for Active Directory Certificate Services enumeration and abuse

name: Coercer
description: Automatically find and exploit Windows authentication coercion vulnerabilities. PetitPotam, PrinterBug, and more.
useCases: NTLM relay attack setup via forced authentication
longDescription: Coercer is an automated tool designed to locate and exploit Windows authentication coercion vulnerabilities. It specifically targets vulnerabilities such as PetitPotam and PrinterBug, which can be exploited to relay NTLM authentication requests. By automating the discovery and exploitation process, Coercer assists attackers and security testers in assessing the security posture of Active Directory environments against these types of threats.
githubDescription: A python script to automatically coerce a Windows server to authenticate on an arbitrary machine through 12 methods.

name: SharpHound
description: Official BloodHound data collector. Enumerates Active Directory objects, sessions, ACLs, and trusts for graph analysis.
useCases: Active Directory data collection for BloodHound
longDescription: SharpHound is the official data collector for BloodHound, a tool used to visualize Active Directory environments. Written in C#, it enumerates Active Directory objects, sessions, access control lists (ACLs), and trust relationships, providing data for graph-based analysis of potential attack paths. SharpHound is essential for red teams seeking to understand and exploit complex Active Directory structures, enhancing strategic attack planning.
githubDescription: C# Data Collector for BloodHound

name: BeEF
description: Browser Exploitation Framework. Hook browsers via XSS, then pivot into the network using browser-based attacks.
longDescription: The Browser Exploitation Framework (BeEF) focuses on the web browser as the attack vector. Unlike other security tools, BeEF looks past the hardened network perimeter and examines exploitability within the context of the browser. Once a browser is hooked (typically via XSS), BeEF can use it to launch further attacks including keylogging, clipboard theft, webcam/mic access, port scanning the internal network, and social engineering attacks — all from within the victim's browser context.
useCases: Browser hooking via XSS for post-exploitation
Internal network scanning through hooked browsers
Credential harvesting via fake login prompts
Social engineering delivery through the browser
githubDescription: The Browser Exploitation Framework Project

name: Social-Engineer Toolkit
description: Open-source social engineering framework. Spear-phishing, web attacks, USB/HID attacks, and credential harvesting.
longDescription: The Social-Engineer Toolkit (SET) is an open-source penetration testing framework designed for social engineering attacks. Created by David Kennedy, it integrates with Metasploit and provides attack vectors including spear-phishing (email), website cloning for credential harvesting, infectious media (USB), mass mailers, and HID attacks. SET automates the creation of believable attack scenarios and is a standard tool in social engineering assessments.
useCases: QR code and wireless attack vectors
githubDescription: The Social-Engineer Toolkit (SET) repository from TrustedSec - All new versions of SET will be deployed here.

name: Quasar RAT
description: Open-source remote administration tool for Windows. Full remote desktop, keylogger, file manager, and reverse proxy.
longDescription: Quasar is a fast and lightweight remote administration tool coded in C#. It provides a stable and easy-to-use user interface with features including remote desktop, file management, keylogging, remote shell, system information gathering, and more. longDescription: puredns is a fast domain resolver and subdomain bruteforcing tool that excels in wildcard detection and DNS validation. It is designed to perform efficient DNS resolution and can handle large datasets, making it suitable for thorough subdomain enumeration tasks. The tool's ability to detect and filter out wildcard DNS responses ensures accurate results, enhancing its reliability in security assessments. puredns's speed and robustness are crucial for security professionals conducting detailed DNS reconnaissance.
githubDescription: Puredns is a fast domain resolver and subdomain bruteforcing tool that can accurately filter out wildcard subdomains and DNS poisoned entries.

name: Findomain
description: Cross-platform subdomain enumerator using certificate transparency logs and multiple APIs for fast passive discovery.
longDescription: Findomain is a cross-platform subdomain enumerator that leverages certificate transparency logs and multiple APIs for swift passive subdomain discovery. It automates the process of gathering subdomains without directly interacting with the target, making it ideal for stealthy reconnaissance. Written in Rust, Findomain is known for its speed and efficiency, providing security researchers with comprehensive subdomain lists for further analysis. Its integration with various data sources enhances its coverage and accuracy.
githubDescription: The fastest and complete solution for domain recognition. Supports screenshoting, port scan, HTTP check, data import from other tools, subdomain monitoring, alerts via Discord, Slack and Telegram, multiple API Keys for sources and much more.

name: dirsearch
description: Mature web path discovery tool with recursive scanning, wordlist-based bruteforcing, and extensive extension support.
longDescription: dirsearch is a mature web path discovery tool that employs recursive scanning and wordlist-based bruteforcing to uncover hidden directories and files on web servers. It supports a wide range of extensions, making it versatile for different web technologies. Security testers use dirsearch to identify misconfigurations and potential entry points in web applications. Its robustness and flexibility make it an essential tool for web application security assessments.
githubDescription: Web path scanner

name: meg
description: Fetch many paths for many hosts concurrently without overloading servers — ideal for large-scale recon on bug bounties.
longDescription: meg is a tool designed to fetch many paths from many hosts concurrently without overwhelming servers, making it ideal for large-scale reconnaissance in bug bounty programs. It operates by queuing requests efficiently, enabling users to probe numerous URLs simultaneously. This capability allows security researchers to quickly gather information about web server configurations and potential vulnerabilities. Its emphasis on concurrency and performance sets it apart as a go-to utility for extensive web path probing.
githubDescription: Fetch many paths for many hosts - without killing the hosts

name: Caido
description: Lightweight and modern web security testing toolkit built in Rust, designed as a fast alternative to Burp Suite.
longDescription: Caido is a modern web security testing toolkit built in Rust, offering a lightweight and fast alternative to traditional tools like Burp Suite. It functions as a web proxy and interceptor, allowing security researchers to analyze, modify, and replay web traffic. Caido's user-friendly interface and high performance make it suitable for both experienced testers and newcomers. Its focus on speed and efficiency makes it a compelling choice for web application security testing.
githubDescription: 🚀 Caido releases, wiki and roadmap

name: Trivy
description: Comprehensive vulnerability scanner for containers, filesystems, git repos, and Kubernetes with SBOM generation.
longDescription: Trivy is a comprehensive vulnerability scanner capable of analyzing containers, filesystems, git repositories, and Kubernetes configurations. It generates Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) and identifies vulnerabilities by matching known CVEs against the scanned components. Designed for ease of use, Trivy integrates seamlessly into CI/CD pipelines, enabling continuous security assessments. Its broad coverage and support for multiple formats make it a versatile tool for maintaining security across diverse environments.
githubDescription: Find vulnerabilities, misconfigurations, secrets, SBOM in containers, Kubernetes, code repositories, clouds and more

name: Grype
description: Vulnerability scanner for container images and filesystems that matches installed packages against known CVEs.
longDescription: Grype is a vulnerability scanner for container images and filesystems that identifies known vulnerabilities by matching installed packages against CVE databases. It provides detailed reports and integrates with SBOM to enhance software supply chain security. Grype's capabilities in scanning and its focus on container security make it an essential tool for DevOps teams and security professionals. Its support for multiple image formats and package managers broadens its applicability in modern development workflows.
githubDescription: A vulnerability scanner for container images and filesystems

name: Semgrep
description: Lightweight static analysis engine for finding bugs and enforcing code standards across 30+ languages with custom rules.
longDescription: Semgrep is a lightweight static analysis engine that helps find bugs and enforce code standards across over 30 programming languages. It uses custom rules to perform code scanning, offering flexibility in detecting vulnerabilities and ensuring best practices. Semgrep's ability to integrate into development environments allows developers to catch issues early in the coding process. Its focus on customizable rules and language support makes it a powerful tool for secure software development.
githubDescription: Lightweight static analysis for many languages. Find bug variants with patterns that look like source code.

name: DalFox
description: Parameter analysis and XSS scanner with automatic payload generation, DOM-based detection, and pipeline support.
longDescription: DalFox is a powerful tool for parameter analysis and detecting cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerabilities. Built in Go, it automates the generation of payloads and supports DOM-based detection techniques, making it suitable for both reflected and stored XSS. The tool's pipeline support allows for seamless integration into testing workflows. DalFox stands out due to its speed and efficiency in identifying complex XSS vectors across various web applications.
githubDescription: 🌙🦊 Dalfox is a powerful open-source XSS scanner and utility focused on automation.

name: Retire.js
description: Scanner for detecting use of JavaScript libraries with known vulnerabilities in web applications.
longDescription: Retire.js is a JavaScript library scanner designed to detect known vulnerabilities in web applications. It identifies outdated and insecure dependencies by checking against a comprehensive database of CVEs and other vulnerability sources. The tool is essential for developers and security professionals seeking to ensure their applications are free from exploitable third-party components. Retire.js is widely used for dependency scanning in the development lifecycle.
githubDescription: scanner detecting the use of JavaScript libraries with known vulnerabilities. Can also generate an SBOM of the libraries it finds.

name: h8mail
description: Email OSINT and password breach hunting tool that queries multiple breach databases and paste sites.
longDescription: h8mail is an open-source tool for conducting OSINT investigations and hunting for compromised email credentials. Written in Python, it queries multiple breach databases and paste sites to uncover leaked passwords and other sensitive information. The tool is highly effective for credential hunting, allowing users to assess the exposure of email accounts across various data breaches. h8mail is valuable for both security professionals and individuals concerned with email security.
githubDescription: Email OSINT & Password breach hunting tool, locally or using premium services. Supports chasing down related email

name: Maryam

longDescription: Maryam is a modular OSINT framework that facilitates comprehensive data gathering from numerous sources. name: sn0int

longDescription: sn0int is a semi-automatic OSINT framework and package manager designed for gathering intelligence on IPs, emails, domains, and individuals. name: socialscan

longDescription: socialscan is a tool for checking the availability of email addresses and usernames across multiple platforms. name: SocialFish

longDescription: SocialFish is a phishing tool focused on social media platforms, providing real-time credential interception and geolocation tracking.
